title: Your $200 Splurge Is a $1,400 Retirement Theft
hook: Every dollar you spend in your thirties is secretly worth seven in retirement — and that last impulse buy just robbed your future self.
angle: Reframes every discretionary purchase as a 7× future cost using the compound interest multiplier — makes the abstract math visceral and personal.
audience: 25-40 year olds who spend freely without connecting present spending to retirement outcomes.
